PREDICTION_UNIT: One row represents one Formula 1 driver at one lap. Predict the probability that this driver will make a pit stop on the next lap.
DOMAIN_CONTEXT: Formula 1 pit-stop timing from lap-level telemetry-like tabular data. The useful signal is likely in tyre compound and age, stint state, lap/race progress, current position, lap-time degradation, and current or past pit-stop state. The target is next-lap pit-stop probability, so causal ordering within each race-driver sequence matters.
AVAILABLE_COLUMNS: Train columns: id, Driver, Compound, Race, Year, PitStop, LapNumber, Stint, TyreLife, Position, LapTime (s), LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, RaceProgress, Position_Change, PitNextLap. Test columns: id, Driver, Compound, Race, Year, PitStop, LapNumber, Stint, TyreLife, Position, LapTime (s), LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, RaceProgress, Position_Change. Submission columns: id, PitNextLap. Categorical/context columns include Driver, Compound, Race, and Year. Numeric lap-state columns include PitStop, LapNumber, Stint, TyreLife, Position, LapTime (s), LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, RaceProgress, and Position_Change.
PREVIOUS_SOLUTION_MODES: Attached AIDE artifacts may include multiple solution styles. Infer each style from the node code, plan, and analysis instead of relying on labels.
VALIDATION_CONTEXT: Submissions are evaluated by ROC AUC, higher is better. Local CV and Kaggle public leaderboard score may diverge, so treat CV-only improvements as suspicious when they are unstable, duplicate-heavy, or based on fragile random splits. Grouped or time-aware validation by race, year, and driver-race may be relevant when evaluating leakage and generalization.
LEAKAGE_CONSTRAINTS: Do not use PitNextLap except as the training target. Do not use future laps, future pit stops, future stint endpoints, next-lap labels, or any feature derived from rows after the prediction row. Current-row test columns are available at prediction time, including current PitStop, but any rolling, cumulative, lag, or sequence feature must be computed using only the current and previous rows within the same race-driver context. Avoid target encoding or empirical target priors unless they are strictly fold-safe inside the generated solution.
EXPERIMENT_BUDGET: Each hypothesis must be verifiable as a single first experiment in under 1 hour on a server-class machine with about 200 GB RAM, 16 CPU cores, and one NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU. Prefer compact feature blocks and bounded model changes. Do not propose first tests that require millions of generated features, exhaustive interaction searches, broad hyperparameter sweeps, very large ensembles, or multi-hour training.
AUTOGLUON_PREPROCESS_EXAMPLE:
```python
def preprocess(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    df = df.copy()
    eps = 1e-6

    compound = df["Compound"].astype("string").str.strip().str.upper()
    tyre_life = pd.to_numeric(df["TyreLife"], errors="coerce").astype(float)
    lap_number = pd.to_numeric(df["LapNumber"], errors="coerce").astype(float)
    race_progress = pd.to_numeric(df["RaceProgress"], errors="coerce").astype(float)

    expected_life = compound.map({
        "SOFT": 18.0,
        "MEDIUM": 25.0,
        "HARD": 35.0,
        "INTERMEDIATE": 20.0,
        "WET": 16.0,
    }).fillna(24.0)

    estimated_total_laps = (lap_number / (race_progress + eps)).clip(
        lower=lap_number,
        upper=250.0,
    )
    estimated_laps_remaining = (estimated_total_laps - lap_number).clip(lower=0.0)

    df["TyreLife_ExpectedLife_Ratio"] = tyre_life / (expected_life + eps)
    df["Expected_Life_Remaining"] = expected_life - tyre_life
    df["CurrentTyre_CanReachFinish"] = (
        estimated_laps_remaining <= (expected_life - tyre_life)
    ).astype("int8")
    return df
```
